name: Forgotten Way Trail
name_original: Quintanaluengos desde Salinas de Pisuerga a orillas del río Pisuerga
description: Hike the moderate 6.7-mile Quintanaluengos trail from Salinas de Pisuerga, following the scenic Pisuerga River and passing historic villages
region_intro: Hiking trails around Salinas de Pisuerga are located in the Montaña Palentina region, offering diverse landscapes for outdoor exploration. The area is characterized by the Cantabrian Mountains, the Pisuerga River, and extensive oak forests. Terrain varies from tranquil river valleys to rugged mountainous areas, providing a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Salinas De Pisuerga The most popular hiking route is Sculptor Ursi's Trail, a 7.1 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This moderate route showcases art installations amidst rural landscapes and oak forests. Another top favourite among local hikers is Forgotten Way Trail, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.8 km) path. This route offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. Local hikers also love the Palentino Romanesque Route, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through river valleys and showcasing Romanesque architecture, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Salinas De Pisuerga is defined by the Cantabrian Mountains, the Pisuerga River, and oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging mountain routes. The routes in Salinas De Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Salinas De Pisuerga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Salinas de Pisuerga?
A: Salinas de Pisuerga offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 50 routes available. These trails cater to different skill levels, ranging from easy riverside paths to more challenging mountain ascents within the Montaña Palentina region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Salinas de Pisuerga?
A: The terrain around Salinas de Pisuerga is quite diverse. You'll find tranquil river valleys along the Pisuerga River, extensive oak forests, and more rugged mountainous areas as you venture into the Cantabrian Mountains. This variety ensures a range of hiking experiences, from gentle strolls to more demanding climbs.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Salinas de Pisuerga has 11 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or families. One example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176686779/ermita-de-santa-eulalia-y-barrio-de-san-pedro-desde-barrio-de-santa-maria-circular-por-el-camino-natural-del-romanico-palentino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Santa Eulalia y Barrio de San Pedro desde Barrio de Santa María&lt;/a&gt;, which is a circular path along the Camino Natural del Románico Palentino, offering a pleasant and accessible experience.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Salinas de Pisuerga?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067879553/sculptor-ursi-s-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sculptor Ursi's Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.1-mile loop that takes you through rural landscapes and oak forests, showcasing art installations. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093126490/gr-1-historical-trail-perapertu-and-el-portillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 1 Historical Trail: Perapertú and El Portillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Hiking around Salinas de Pisuerga offers views of the Cantabrian Mountains, the Pisuerga River, and beautiful oak forests. You can also discover nearby bodies of water like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its rich Romanesque heritage, with routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176650029/palentino-romanesque-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palentino Romanesque Route&lt;/a&gt; leading past historical sites.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sights accessible from the trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can visit the 16th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt; over the Pisuerga River, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3306399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Cecilia&lt;/a&gt;, a 12th-century construction. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103958803/forgotten-way-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forgotten Way Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: Is Salinas de Pisuerga a good destination for dog-friendly hikes?
A: The natural environment of Salinas de Pisuerga, with its extensive forests and riverbanks, is generally welcoming for dogs. Many trails, especially those through oak forests and along the Pisuerga River, are suitable for walks with your canine companion. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local regulations regarding pets in natural parks.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Salinas de Pisuerga?
A: The Montaña Palentina region, where Salinas de Pisuerga is located, offers pleasant hiking conditions from spring through autumn. Spring brings lush greenery and wildflowers, while autumn showcases vibrant fall foliage in the oak forests. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer, especially in open areas. Winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for snow and colder temperatures in higher elevations.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Salinas de Pisuerga?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Salinas de Pisuerga, especially near trailheads or in the town center. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for designated parking areas. Some highlights, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3306408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Good place to spend the night&lt;/a&gt; near the Aguilar Reservoir, also indicate parking options.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads?
A: While Salinas de Pisuerga is accessible by public transport, direct connections to specific remote trailheads might be limited. It's best to plan your journey by checking local bus schedules to Salinas de Pisuerga itself. From the town, many trails are within walking distance, or you might consider local taxi services for more distant starting points.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Salinas de Pisuerga?
A: The hiking routes in Salinas de Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the blend of natural beauty with cultural and historical points of interest, such as the art installations on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067879553/sculptor-ursi-s-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sculptor Ursi's Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate, the Montaña Palentina region offers more challenging options. With mountains exceeding 2500 meters in altitude, experienced hikers can find routes with significant elevation gain and rugged terrain. There are 3 difficult routes recorded in the area, providing a good test for those seeking a more strenuous adventure.

Q: Can I combine hiking with other activities in the area?
A: Beyond hiking, the Salinas de Pisuerga area offers opportunities for other outdoor activities. The nearby Aguilar and Cervera de Pisuerga Reservoirs are suitable for non-motorized water sports in summer. Equestrian centers provide guided horseback riding tours through the Montaña Palentina, and several routes, like the "Ruta BTT - Pedaleando por el Románico Palentino," are also popular for cycling.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail begins in Salinas de Pisuerga. You can typically find parking within the village, which serves as a convenient starting point for the route along the Pisuerga River.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally suitable for dogs. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock, and remember to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed in various seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, with autumn providing vibrant colors along the riverside. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: This moderate hike is approximately 10.8 kilometers long and typically takes about 2 hours and 49 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a mix of surfaces, including dedicated paths, streets, and roads. You'll encounter natural, paved, and unpaved sections. While generally accessible, some parts may require sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll primarily follow the picturesque Pisuerga River, passing through charming villages like Barcenilla and Ligüérzana. A notable landmark is the 16th-century medieval bridge in Salinas de Pisuerga. The route also offers glimpses of local architecture and Romanesque art in the surrounding villages.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: The description suggests a route from Salinas de Pisuerga to Quintanaluengos and back, implying it can be done as an out-and-back or a circular route if you plan your return. The path often runs directly next to the Pisuerga River, with opportunities to cross via footbridges.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other well-known paths. You'll find it overlaps with parts of the Camino Olvidado (Matamorisca-Puente Almuhey), Románico Palentino, and the Camino Natural del Románico Palentino. Ruta del Carbón de cok. Etapa 3.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll pass through villages such as Salinas de Pisuerga, Barcenilla, and Ligüérzana. These villages, particularly Salinas de Pisuerga, may offer local establishments where you can find refreshments or a meal.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered a moderate hike. It's suitable for individuals with good fitness levels, featuring easily accessible paths, though some sections may require careful footing.
